1	# Chester Thinking Lessons
2	
3	| Score | Category | Lesson | Context |
4	|---|---|---|---|
5	| 7 | — | Before proposing a new architecture, check whether the target architecture already exists in the codebase with incomplete migration | Architecture review / validation redesign, severity consolidation, service error redesign, entity authoring attributes, Compiler contract: canonical form already existed as GUID-in-text, Pipeline formalization: pipe already existed as service model, problem was leaked orchestration, Semantic validation ownership: bridge + semantic rules infrastructure existed but were incomplete — agent concluded "nothing needs to change" instead of recognizing incompleteness |
6	| 5 | — | When the user rejects a proposed solution, the problem statement itself likely needs reframing — don't iterate on solutions, re-examine the problem | Design discovery where proposed solution doesn't fit, problem statement framing debates, review pipeline decomposition corrected twice, entity authoring attributes design brief rejected, Pipeline formalization: five alternatives all suboptimal — structural flaw was elsewhere |
7	| 4 | — | When the user has a feeling something is wrong but no concrete evidence, treat it as a design signal worth investigating — it often leads to foundational insights | Early-stage design discovery, review pipeline token cost concern, entity authoring attributes hidden architectural constraints, Semantic validation ownership: designer directed investigation that revealed incomplete system when agent had concluded no action needed |
8	| 3 | — | Dependency graph constraints can force duplication that looks like an architectural choice — examine the graph before blaming the code | Validation system analysis, diagnostic code design, Validation rule placement: dependency inversion was a known deferred issue, checks placed in wrong project because the dependency arrow allowed it |
9	| 3 | — | Don't add configuration dials (profiles, levels, modes) when the system can self-adjust — the user will cut them | Pipeline design where depth profiles were unnecessary; subagent model cut in favor of simpler MCP approach; Pipeline formalization: carrier modes, service bus, middleware chain all eliminated as unnecessary over DI service model |
10	| 3 | — | Stay focused on the problem being solved — don't let secondary concerns (future extensibility, edge cases, unused infrastructure) expand the design beyond what's needed | Design interviews where analysis of challenges inflated scope beyond the original problem, Templates inflated scope in entity authoring attributes, Missing semantic rules: designer identified code review issues as mechanical execution and sprint phasing as specification, not design — kept interview focused on design-level decisions |
11	| 1 | — | A problem statement must describe the pain, not prescribe the solution — if the agent keeps embedding solutions, the user must write it themselves | Entity authoring attributes: five problem statement revisions, agent couldn't separate problem from solution |
12	| 3 | — | Assess the user's degree of certainty when weighing responses. "I am not sure" or "probably" is not a strong signal and should not be taken as fact. "This is", "Use this", "The X is Y" are strong signals and should be weighted accordingly | Entity authoring attributes: "probably" taken as agreement when it indicated unresolved concerns about codebase impact, presentation layer, and extensibility. Compiler contract: "not sure" and "no idea" correctly identified session scope limits, Missing semantic rules: "not sure" on granularity correctly treated as open question requiring more information, not as agreement |
13	| 1 | — | When the user says "simple," take that literally — start with the most mechanical approach (append to file) before considering structured alternatives | Feature design where capture/logging is involved |
14	| 1 | — | Let the user's existing experience of the tool guide the output format — mirror what they already see rather than restructuring it | Designing output artifacts |
15	| 2 | — | Check approved architecture documents before exploring alternatives — they may eliminate options the problem statement presents as open | Design discovery where TDRs/ADRs constrain the solution space, Validation rule placement: TDR-ARCH-100/ARCH-105 eliminated "keep in Domain" option entirely |
16	| 1 | — | If approved documents constrain the design space, surface those constraints to the designer — they need to understand the constraint and may choose to revise the foundational document | Design discovery where existing TDRs interact with new requirements |
17	| 1 | — | Architectural compliance does not imply functional completeness — a system can pass every constraint check while producing no output; use behavioral analysis (what happens at runtime) alongside structural analysis (what the dependency graph allows) | Semantic validation ownership: code review found zero violations and full compliance, but bridge was a no-op and semantic rules never invoked in production |
18	| 1 | — | When a system works as an aggregate, don't assume its parts are independently rearrangeable — restructuring must preserve the emergent whole, not just relocate individual pieces | Review pipeline redesign where user corrected decomposition assumptions twice |
19	| 1 | — | When a sophisticated architecture fails on user experience, don't patch it — ask what the simplest version that preserves the core value looks like | Subagent pipeline replaced by MCP tool calls after real-session pacing failure |
20	| 1 | — | Tool calls enforce discipline better than prompt instructions — if the agent must do something every round, make it a tool dependency not a written rule | MCP hidden engine design where scoring discipline was the core requirement |
21	| 1 | — | Redirect the agent's goal rather than prohibiting behavior — "understand this" channels energy more effectively than "don't conclude yet" | Architect Round 1 collapse: prohibitions against problem statement production failed because the agent's completion drive overrode them |
22	| 1 | — | When a contrarian challenge threatens to eliminate a design element, test its justification through analogy to established systems — if no real-world analogue supports it, the element may be unnecessary | Compiler contract: IL analogy answered the contrarian challenge about whether the canonical form was justified as a third representation |
23	| 1 | — | When the designer provides custom evaluation weights, they reveal priorities the agent's defaults missed — let the designer own the metric weighting rather than defending agent-chosen weights | Compiler contract: user's VE-35% weighting jumped Option A from last to second, revealing validation expressiveness mattered more than agent assumed |
24	| 1 | — | When all design alternatives feel suboptimal, look for a structural flaw in the system that's confounding the analysis — the problem may not be what to build but what to fix | Pipeline formalization: five alternatives all suboptimal because orchestration had leaked from Logic into Desktop; fixing the layer violation made the simplest option work naturally |
25	| 1 | — | Don't ask the designer questions the codebase can answer — look it up first, then present findings for confirmation | Missing semantic rules: agent asked about containment relationships when entity DTOs answer this directly; designer challenged "why are you asking that question?" |
26

file.content:
---
name: Working directory model for design artifacts
description: Design artifacts saved to a single gitignored plans directory (docs/chester/plans/) outside worktrees — finish skills copy final docs into worktree for merge
type: project
---

As of 2026-04-07, Chester uses a single gitignored directory for all working artifacts during design/specification phases.

**Current model (collapsed from earlier dual-directory design):**
- `docs/chester/plans/` — gitignored directory where ALL working documents live during design, specification, and planning
- Sprint subdirectories: `YYYYMMDD-##-word-word-word-word/` with `design/`, `spec/`, `plan/`, `summary/` subdirs
- When work finishes, finish skills copy final artifacts into the worktree's plans path for merge into git history

**Why:** Mike found navigating into worktrees to find documents cumbersome. A fixed, known location outside the worktree means the user always knows where to look during review. Final documents are committed alongside code for historical traceability.

**How to apply:** Skills that write artifacts should always use `$CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` (resolved via `eval "$(chester-config-read)"`). Never hardcode paths. The plans directory is gitignored — only finish skills handle the copy-to-worktree step.

The design brief and thinking summary are written to `docs/chester/working/20260408-02-artifact-directory-worktree-clarity/design/` — following the very model this sprint is about to implement. The worktree at `.worktrees/20260408-02-artifact-directory-worktree-clarity` is ready for code changes. No documents were committed there.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Design phase complete. Artifacts written to working/:
- `design/artifact-directory-worktree-clarity-thinking-00.md`
- `design/artifact-directory-worktree-clarity-design-00.md`

Ready to move to spec. Invoking design-specify.

Base directory for this skill: /home/mike/.claude/plugins/cache/ordinarymoose/chester/1.0.0/skills/design-specify

# Build Spec

Formalize an approved design into a durable spec document, validate it through automated and human review.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke plan-build or any implementation skill until the spec has passed automated review AND the user has approved it. Only then proceed to invoke plan-build.
</HARD-GATE>

## Entry Condition

A design exists — either:
- A design brief from design-figure-out at `{CHESTER_WORKING_DIR}/{sprint-subdir}/design/{sprint-name}-design-00.md`
- A human-written brief or design from an external source
- A design described in conversation context

The working directory and subdirectories should already exist (created by figure-out). If invoked standalone, this skill creates them.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Setup** — if invoked standalone (no figure-out), invoke `start-bootstrap`; otherwise sprint context already exists
2. **Read design brief** — read the design brief from disk or gather design from conversation context
3. **Competing architectures** — dispatch 3 `feature-dev:code-architect` agents with different trade-off lenses; present approaches to user; user picks direction
4. **Write spec document** — synthesize design into structured spec based on chosen architecture (see `util-artifact-schema` for output path and naming)
5. **Automated spec review loop** — dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent with design brief, Think Tool gate per issue, fix and re-dispatch (max 2 iterations, then escalate to user)
6. **User review gate** — present clean spec to user for review; if changes requested, apply and loop back to step 5
7. **Transition** — invoke plan-build (spec is NOT committed here — `finish-archive-artifacts` copies all artifacts into the worktree for merge)

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph build_spec {
    "Standalone?" [shape=diamond];
    "Ask output directory\nCreate subdirectories" [shape=box];
    "Read design brief" [shape=box];
    "Dispatch 3 architect agents\n(minimal, clean, pragmatic)" [shape=box];
    "Present approaches\nUser picks direction" [shape=diamond];
    "Write spec document" [shape=box];
    "Dispatch spec reviewer" [shape=box];
    "Issues found?" [shape=diamond];
    "Think Tool per issue\nFix issues" [shape=box];
    "Iteration < 2?" [shape=diamond];
    "Escalate to user" [shape=box];
    "Present to user" [shape=box];
    "User approves?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke plan-build" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Standalone?" -> "Ask output directory\nCreate subdirectories" [label="yes"];
    "Standalone?" -> "Read design brief" [label="no"];
    "Ask output directory\nCreate subdirectories" -> "Read design brief";
    "Read design brief" -> "Dispatch 3 architect agents\n(minimal, clean, pragmatic)";
    "Dispatch 3 architect agents\n(minimal, clean, pragmatic)" -> "Present approaches\nUser picks direction";
    "Present approaches\nUser picks direction" -> "Write spec document";
    "Write spec document" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer";
    "Dispatch spec reviewer" -> "Issues found?";
    "Issues found?" -> "Present to user" [label="no — approved"];
    "Issues found?" -> "Think Tool per issue\nFix issues" [label="yes"];
    "Think Tool per issue\nFix issues" -> "Iteration < 2?";
    "Iteration < 2?" -> "Dispatch spec reviewer" [label="yes"];
    "Iteration < 2?" -> "Escalate to user" [label="no"];
    "Escalate to user" -> "Present to user";
    "Present to user" -> "User approves?";
    "User approves?" -> "Invoke plan-build" [label="yes (spec stays in working dir)"];
    "User approves?" -> "Write spec document" [label="changes requested"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking plan-build.** Do NOT invoke any other implementation skill.

## Standalone Invocation

When invoked without a prior design-figure-out session, invoke `start-bootstrap` to
set up the sprint context (config, naming, directories, task reset).

## Competing Architectures

After reading the design brief but before writing the spec, dispatch three `feature-dev:code-architect` agents in parallel. Each receives the same design brief and codebase context but is constrained to a different trade-off profile:

| Agent | Lens | Prompt guidance |
|-------|------|-----------------|
| Architect 1 | **Minimal changes** | "Design an architecture for [design brief summary] that minimizes the diff. Maximize reuse of existing code, patterns, and infrastructure. Smallest possible change surface." |
| Architect 2 | **Clean architecture** | "Design an architecture for [design brief summary] that optimizes for maintainability and clarity. Use clean abstractions, clear boundaries, and future-proof the design — even if it means more upfront work." |
| Architect 3 | **Pragmatic balance** | "Design an architecture for [design brief summary] that balances implementation speed with code quality. Make pragmatic trade-offs — don't gold-plate, but don't cut corners that will hurt in 6 months." |

Each architect returns a complete blueprint with patterns found, architecture decision, component design, implementation map, data flow, and build sequence.

**Present the comparison to the user:**

1. Summarize each approach in 3–5 sentences — what it does differently and why
2. Compare trade-offs in a table: change surface, maintainability, implementation effort, risk
3. State your recommendation with reasoning — which approach best fits the design brief's goals and constraints
4. Ask the user which approach they prefer, or whether they want a hybrid

The user's choice (or hybrid direction) becomes the architectural foundation for the spec. If the user says "whatever you think," go with your recommendation but state it explicitly so the choice is on record.

This step exists because humans evaluate *comparisons* far better than *single proposals*. Presenting one architecture and asking "is this good?" is a weaker gate than presenting three and asking "which trade-off profile fits?"

## Writing the Spec

- Read the design brief from disk (if it exists) and conversation context
- Using the user's chosen architecture as the structural foundation, synthesize into a structured spec document covering: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing strategy, constraints, non-goals
- Scale each section to its complexity — a few sentences if straightforward, detailed if nuanced
- No YAML frontmatter is needed in spec documents. All skills read output paths from the project config, not from document frontmatter.
- Write to the `spec/` subdirectory (see `util-artifact-schema` for exact path and naming)

## Automated Spec Review Loop

**Review purpose: Design Alignment** — does the spec faithfully address the design brief's goals, constraints, and decisions?

After writing the spec:

1. Dispatch spec-document-reviewer subagent (see spec-reviewer.md in this skill directory)
   - Provide both the spec path AND the design brief path
   - If no design brief exists (standalone invocation), dispatch with spec only — the reviewer falls back to internal-consistency checking
2. The reviewer checks: goals coverage, constraints respected, no untraceable additions, internal consistency

**think gate (per issue):** When the spec reviewer returns issues, ask this question, think
about the results, and implement the findings:
  "Is this issue valid given the spec's stated intent? What is the minimal fix?
   Does this fix affect any adjacent section of the spec?"

Apply the fix, then move to the next issue. Re-dispatch the reviewer after all issues from the current round are addressed.

3. If loop exceeds 2 iterations, escalate to user for guidance
4. On subsequent iterations, increment the version number (see `util-artifact-schema` for versioning)

## User Review Gate

After the automated review loop passes:

> "Spec written and reviewed at `{path}`. Please review and let me know if you want changes before we proceed to the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, apply them and re-run the automated review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

## Post-Approval

After the user approves the spec, it remains in the working directory. The spec is NOT committed here — `finish-archive-artifacts` copies all artifacts into the worktree for merge.

## MCP Usage

- **Think** only — per issue evaluation during the review loop
- Sequential and Structured thinking are not used; spec writing is craft, and the review loop volume does not warrant structured cross-referencing

## Integration

- **Calls:** `start-bootstrap` (standalone only)
- **Reads:** `util-artifact-schema` (naming/paths), `util-budget-guard`
- **Invoked by:** design-figure-out (primary), or user directly (standalone)
- **Transitions to:** plan-build
- **Does NOT invoke:** plan-attack, plan-smell, or any implementation skill

file.content:
# Design Brief: Artifact Directory Worktree Clarity

**Sprint:** 20260408-02-artifact-directory-worktree-clarity
**Date:** 2026-04-08
**Status:** Approved

## Problem

Chester's artifact directory model behaves inconsistently across sessions. Skills write to different locations, the config exports two directory paths that are used interchangeably, and a half-completed migration left the system in a contradictory state. The user can't reliably predict where documents are.

## Design Decisions

### D1: Single write target during active work

All skills write exclusively to `docs/chester/working/{sprint}/` (gitignored). No skill writes to `plans/` during any phase of active work — design, spec, plan, execute, or summary. Working/ is the one source of truth for the user.

**Rationale:** Eliminates the "where is my document?" question entirely. There is exactly one answer at every point in the pipeline.

### D2: Worktree is for code only

The worktree is created at its current timing (end of design-figure-out) and is used exclusively for code changes during execution. No documents are committed to the worktree until merge time.

**Rationale:** Documents and worktrees are orthogonal concerns. Coupling them created confusion about what lives where.

### D3: Archive is a single atomic operation at merge time

When the user is ready to close the worktree and merge, all documents are copied from `working/{sprint}/` into the worktree's `plans/{sprint}/` directory and committed. This is the only moment `plans/` is created or written to.

**Rationale:** Single point of document-to-git transfer. No intermediate states, no partial commits, no ambiguity.

### D4: No mid-pipeline checkpoint commits of documents

Current checkpoint commits (e.g., "checkpoint: design complete" in the worktree) are eliminated. Documents exist only in working/ until the archive step.

**Rationale:** Working/ is the source of truth during active work. Checkpoint commits added complexity without value.

### D5: Working directory is user-managed scratch

After merge, `working/{sprint}/` remains on disk as acceptable debris. The user deletes it when they choose. No automated cleanup.

**Rationale:** Simplicity. No lifecycle management, no retention policies, no cleanup automation.

### D6: Plans directory is the permanent git record

`plans/{sprint}/` exists only in git history, created at merge time. It's the committed copy of what was in `working/`.

**Rationale:** Git history is the permanent record. Plans/ is a commit artifact, not a working artifact.

## What Changes

- **Config system**: Skills only need `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR` during active work. `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` is only relevant to the archive step.
- **Every skill that writes artifacts**: Must write to `working/`, never to `plans/`.
- **Checkpoint commits in worktree**: Removed from design-figure-out and any other skill that commits documents mid-pipeline.
- **finish-archive-artifacts**: Becomes the single point where documents enter the worktree — copy from `working/` to worktree's `plans/`, commit, then proceed to merge.

## What Doesn't Change

- Worktree creation timing
- Sprint naming convention (YYYYMMDD-##-word-word-word-word)
- Document naming convention (design-00.md, spec-00.md, etc.)
- Subdirectory structure within a sprint (design/, spec/, plan/, summary/)

## Scope Boundary

This design covers the directory model and document lifecycle only. It does not address:
- Plugin cache staleness (separate operational concern)
- Skill content beyond artifact path references
- Config variable naming or API changes beyond what's needed for the directory model

input.description: Minimal changes architect
input.prompt:
Design an architecture for the following change that minimizes the diff. Maximize reuse of existing code, patterns, and infrastructure. Smallest possible change surface.

## Context

This is the Chester project at /home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester — a skill-based development workflow framework for Claude Code. Skills are Markdown files in skills/ that define workflows. A config script (chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh) exports directory paths. Skills write design documents, specs, plans, and summaries to directories on disk.

## Design Brief Summary

Chester's artifact directory model needs to be simplified:

1. **Single write target**: All skills write exclusively to `docs/chester/working/{sprint}/` (gitignored). No skill writes to `plans/` during active work.
2. **Worktree is for code only**: No documents committed to worktree until merge time.
3. **Archive at merge only**: Documents copied from `working/{sprint}/` to worktree's `plans/{sprint}/` only when closing the worktree.
4. **No checkpoint commits of documents**: Eliminate mid-pipeline checkpoint commits in worktree.
5. **Working/ is user-managed scratch**: No automated cleanup.
6. **Plans/ is the permanent git record**: Created only at merge time.

## What to analyze

Read these files to understand the current state:
- chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh (config system)
- skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md (current artifact schema)
- skills/design-figure-out/SKILL.md (writes design artifacts, creates worktree)
- skills/design-specify/SKILL.md (writes specs)
- skills/plan-build/SKILL.md (writes plans)
- skills/execute-write/SKILL.md (executes plans)
- skills/finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md (archive step)
- skills/finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md (merge step)
- skills/finish-write-records/SKILL.md (writes summaries)
- .gitignore

Provide a complete blueprint: which files need to change, what changes in each, patterns to follow, and a build sequence. Focus on the SMALLEST possible diff that achieves the design goals.

### What the Design Brief Is Asking For

In summary: eliminate any skill behavior that writes documents to `plans/` during active work. The only path to `plans/` is through `finish-archive-artifacts` at merge time. No mid-pipeline checkpoint commits of documents in worktrees.

### Patterns and Conventions Found

**Current two-directory model description appears in three places that must all change together:**

1. `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh` — line 7 comment: "CHESTER_PLANS_DIR — relative to repo root, tracked in git, for final artifacts merged with code". The code itself is correct — `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` is just a variable. No code change needed, only the comment.

2. `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md` — lines 26-39: describes both directories to skills. This is the primary schema document that every skill reads. This is the biggest change.

3. `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/setup-start/SKILL.md` — lines 47-101: first-run setup explains the two-directory model to the user and configures both paths. This must be updated but the config structure itself stays.

**Skills that reference `plans/` directly or describe archival behavior:**

- `design-figure-out/SKILL.md` line 556: "Design artifacts stay in the working directory — `finish-archive-artifacts` copies them into the worktree for merge." — this is already correct.
- `design-figure-out/SKILL.md` line 563: "finish-archive-artifacts copies them into the worktree for permanent history." — already correct.
- `design-specify/SKILL.md` line 69: "(spec stays in working dir)" — already correct.
- `design-specify/SKILL.md` line 143: "The spec is NOT committed here — `finish-archive-artifacts` copies all artifacts into the worktree for merge." — already correct.
- `plan-build/SKILL.md` line 49: "The plan is NOT committed here — `finish-archive-artifacts` copies all artifacts into the worktree for merge." — already correct.
- `plan-build/SKILL.md` line 187: "The plan remains in the working directory (gitignored). `finish-archive-artifacts` copies all artifacts into the worktree for merge." — already correct.
- `execute-write/SKILL.md` lines 178-186: the finish sequence invokes `finish-write-records` → `finish-archive-artifacts` → `finish-close-worktree` — already correct.
- `finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md`: entire skill is unchanged — it's the correct mechanism.
- `finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md`: unchanged — it executes after archive.
- `finish-write-records/SKILL.md` line 206: "Feature mode artifacts stay in the working directory — `finish-archive-artifacts` handles committing them." — already correct.

**The actual problem: `setup-start` teaches users the two-directory model incorrectly.** Lines 47-101 describe it as a two-directory model where documents eventually land in `plans/` alongside code at worktree-close time. The language is not wrong per se, but it implies `plans/` is an active destination skill-writers should think about. The new model is: `plans/` is a merge-time byproduct, invisible to active work.

**The other problem: `util-artifact-schema` co-describes both directories at the same level of importance** (lines 26-39). The new model should demote `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` to a footnote — it's only relevant to `finish-archive-artifacts`.

**Checkpoint commits:** No skill currently does mid-pipeline checkpoint commits of documents to `plans/`. The design brief's concern #4 (eliminate mid-pipeline checkpoint commits) is already satisfied by the existing skill text. The `finish-archive-artifacts` skill is the single committer. However `setup-start` and `util-artifact-schema` teach the model in a way that could lead a future skill author to add such commits.

### Architecture Decision

The change is a **documentation and mental model correction**, not a code change. The config script, the archive skill, the close skill — all are already correct. What needs to change is how the model is described to skills and users.

Three-file targeted edit:

1. `util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md` — restructure the "Config Resolution" section to present `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR` as the single active target and demote `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` to a "write-once at merge" note.
2. `setup-start/SKILL.md` — rewrite the first-run setup explanation to describe the model correctly: working dir is where all work happens, plans dir is created at merge time.
3. `chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh` — update the line 7-8 comment to match the new mental model.

No other skills need changes. Every pipeline skill already says "this stays in working dir, `finish-archive-artifacts` archives it." That language is correct and reinforces the right model.

### Component Design

**File 1: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md`**

Responsibilities: single source of truth for artifact conventions. The Config Resolution section currently presents both variables at equal weight as co-equal destinations. Change:

- Rename the section from "Config Resolution" to "Config Resolution and Directory Model"
- Rewrite the two-variable table and prose to make clear: `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR` is where every skill writes, `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` is where `finish-archive-artifacts` copies at merge time — no skill other than `finish-archive-artifacts` ever touches `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR`
- Add an explicit rule: "No skill writes to `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` directly. It is populated once, at merge time, by `finish-archive-artifacts`."
- Remove the phrase "where finished artifacts are committed alongside code" as a description of routine work — that framing implies it's a destination skills navigate to

**File 2: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/setup-start/SKILL.md`**

Responsibilities: first-run setup and returning-session verification. The first-run explanation (lines 47-67) presents the two-directory model as two equally active destinations. Change:

- Rewrite the first-run explanation block (lines 47-58) to describe the model as: "all work happens in the working directory; at merge time, documents are archived to plans/"
- The config still stores both paths (that's correct), but the user explanation changes
- The verification checks (Check 2 and Check 3) remain — gitignore enforcement is still correct behavior
- The announcement format stays the same — both paths echoed — so users can see misconfiguration

**File 3: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh`**

Responsibilities: exports path variables. Change:

- Lines 7-8: update the comment to say `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` is the merge-time archive target, not an active write destination

### Implementation Map

**`/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md` — lines 19-39**

Replace the Config Resolution section. Current text says:

```
- **Working directory** — where artifacts live while you're actively working. Gitignored.
  Design briefs, specs, and plans are written here during development. This directory
  lives outside worktrees so documents are always in the same place during review.
- **Plans directory** — where finished artifacts are committed alongside code. Tracked in git.
  The `finish-archive-artifacts` skill copies from working to plans when work is complete.
```

New text for that block:

```
This exports two variables:

| Variable | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR` | Absolute path to gitignored scratch space — all skills write here | `/home/user/project/docs/chester/working` |
| `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` | Relative path to the tracked archive — populated only at merge time | `docs/chester/plans` |

## Directory Model

All artifact writes during a sprint go exclusively to `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR`. No skill
other than `finish-archive-artifacts` writes to or reads from `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR`.

- **Working directory** — the single write target for all pipeline work. Gitignored.
  Every skill in the design → spec → plan → execute pipeline writes here. This directory
  lives outside worktrees so documents persist across branch switches and remain in the
  same location throughout the session.
- **Plans directory** — a merge-time archive, not an active destination. Tracked in git.
  `finish-archive-artifacts` copies the entire sprint subdirectory from working to plans
  exactly once, at the end of the sprint, immediately before closing the worktree.
  No other skill touches this directory.
```

Also update the `CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH` paragraph that follows — remove "either run `setup-start` first or use the defaults (`docs/chester/working/` and `docs/chester/plans/`)" since it implies both directories are setup prerequisites when only the working directory matters for active work.

**`/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/setup-start/SKILL.md` — lines 47-67**

Replace the first-run explanation block. Current text:

```
Chester uses two directories during development:

1. **Working directory** — a gitignored scratch space where design briefs, specs,
   and plans live while you're actively working on them. This stays outside of
   worktrees so you always know where to find your documents during review.

2. **Plans directory** — a tracked directory inside your repository where finished
   artifacts are committed alongside your code, creating a permanent record of the
   design and planning process.
```

New text:

```
Chester uses one active directory and one archive:

1. **Working directory** — a gitignored scratch space where all documents live during
   active work (design briefs, specs, plans, summaries). This stays outside worktrees
   so documents are always in the same location regardless of which branch is checked
   out. Every pipeline skill writes here. Nothing is committed from here mid-sprint.

2. **Plans directory** — a tracked directory where documents are archived at merge time.
   You never write here directly. When a sprint closes, `finish-archive-artifacts`
   copies the working directory's sprint folder into plans and commits it alongside the
   code, creating a permanent record.
```

The config writing step (d through g) stays identical — both paths are still configured. The verification checks stay identical. The announcement format stays identical.

**`/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh` — lines 6-8**

Replace:

```bash
# Two-directory model:
#   CHESTER_WORKING_DIR — absolute path, gitignored, for active design/spec/plan work
#   CHESTER_PLANS_DIR   — relative to repo root, tracked in git, for final artifacts merged with code
```

With:

```bash
# Directory model:
#   CHESTER_WORKING_DIR — absolute path, gitignored; all pipeline skills write here
#   CHESTER_PLANS_DIR   — relative to repo root, tracked in git; populated once at merge time
#                         by finish-archive-artifacts. No other skill touches this directory.
```

### Data Flow

The data flow after this change is clarified (not changed):

```
design-figure-out    → writes design/, thinking/, process/  → CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/
design-specify       → writes spec/                          → CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/
plan-build           → writes plan/, plan-threat-report/     → CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/
execute-write        → writes plan/deferred/                 → CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/
finish-write-records → writes summary/, audit/               → CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/
finish-archive-artifacts → cp -r working/{sprint}/ → worktree/$CHESTER_PLANS_DIR/{sprint}/
                         → git add + git commit
finish-close-worktree → merge or PR → worktree removed
```

`CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` appears exactly once in that flow: inside `finish-archive-artifacts`.

### Build Sequence

- [ ] **Phase 1: Config script comment** — edit `chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh` lines 6-8. Three lines, no logic change.

- [ ] **Phase 2: Artifact schema rewrite** — edit `skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md` Config Resolution section (lines 19-39 approximately). Rewrite the two bullet points and the variable table. Add the "No skill writes to `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` directly" rule. Keep everything else in the file unchanged (Sprint Naming, Directory Layout, File Naming, Versioning, Refactor Artifacts, Inheriting Sprint Context).

- [ ] **Phase 3: Setup-start first-run explanation** — edit `skills/setup-start/SKILL.md` lines 47-67. Rewrite the numbered list explaining the two directories. Keep all config-writing steps, verification checks, and the announcement format unchanged.

- [ ] **Phase 4: Verify cross-references are consistent** — scan for any remaining references to `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` in skill files other than `finish-archive-artifacts`. Expected: zero hits outside that one skill and `util-artifact-schema`. If found, update.

- [ ] **Phase 5: Commit** — single commit: `fix: clarify artifact directory model — working dir is the only active write target`

### Critical Details

**What does NOT change:**

- `finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md` — already correct, do not touch
- `finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md` — already correct, do not touch
- `finish-write-records/SKILL.md` — already correct, do not touch
- `design-figure-out/SKILL.md`, `design-specify/SKILL.md`, `plan-build/SKILL.md`, `execute-write/SKILL.md` — all already say "stays in working dir, `finish-archive-artifacts` archives it." No change needed.
- `chester-config-read.sh` logic — variables, resolution order, jq path, export statements — all unchanged
- `.claude/settings.chester.local.json` structure — both keys remain, no config format change
- `.gitignore` — `docs/chester/working/` is already listed, plans is not listed. Correct.

**Sync requirement:** `util-artifact-schema` is the schema authority. After editing it, verify `setup-start` uses consistent language. The two files describe the same model to different audiences (skills vs. users); they must not contradict each other.

**Refactor artifacts are unchanged:** `finish-write-records` in refactor mode writes directly to `docs/refactor/{slug}/` and commits. That path bypasses both `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR` and `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` entirely. The design brief doesn't touch this path and neither should this change.

**No test changes needed:** The tests in `tests/` validate hooks, config resolution, and budget guards. None test artifact directory routing behavior at the skill text level.

### Relevant Files

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh` — comment update only, lines 6-8
- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md` — Config Resolution section rewrite, lines ~19-39

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/.gitignore` — no changes needed

```

design-specify → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/spec/
plan-build → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/plan/
execute-write → code commits in .worktrees/{sprint}/
execute-verify-complete → empty checkpoint commit in .worktrees/{sprint}/
finish-write-records → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/summary/
finish-archive-artifacts → copies WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/ → WORKTREE/plans/{sprint}/, commits
finish-close-worktree → merges/PRs .worktrees/{sprint}/ to main, removes worktree
```

Plans dir receives content exactly once: at the `finish-archive-artifacts` step. This is the only commit of documents to the worktree. All six design goals are satisfied by this flow.

**`setup-start` Check 3** (verifying plans dir is not gitignored) is correct behavior — if plans/ gets accidentally added to `.gitignore`, the archive commit would silently fail. The check is a safety net, not a contradiction of the model.

Design an architecture for the following change that minimizes the diff. Maximize reuse of existing code, patterns, and infrastructure. Smallest possible change surface.

## Context

This is the Chester project at /home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester — a skill-based development workflow framework for Claude Code. Skills are Markdown files in skills/ that define workflows. A config script (chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh) exports directory paths. Skills write design documents, specs, plans, and summaries to directories on disk.

## Design Brief Summary

Chester's artifact directory model needs to be simplified:

1. **Single write target**: All skills write exclusively to `docs/chester/working/{sprint}/` (gitignored). No skill writes to `plans/` during active work.
2. **Worktree is for code only**: No documents committed to worktree until merge time.
3. **Archive at merge only**: Documents copied from `working/{sprint}/` to worktree's `plans/{sprint}/` only when closing the worktree.
4. **No checkpoint commits of documents**: Eliminate mid-pipeline checkpoint commits in worktree.
5. **Working/ is user-managed scratch**: No automated cleanup.
6. **Plans/ is the permanent git record**: Created only at merge time.

## What to analyze

Read these files to understand the current state:
- chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh (config system)
- skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md (current artifact schema)
- skills/design-figure-out/SKILL.md (writes design artifacts, creates worktree)
- skills/design-specify/SKILL.md (writes specs)
- skills/plan-build/SKILL.md (writes plans)
- skills/execute-write/SKILL.md (executes plans)
- skills/finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md (archive step)
- skills/finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md (merge step)
- skills/finish-write-records/SKILL.md (writes summaries)
- .gitignore

Provide a complete blueprint: which files need to change, what changes in each, patterns to follow, and a build sequence. Focus on the SMALLEST possible diff that achieves the design goals.

## Architecture Blueprint: Chester Artifact Directory Model Simplification

### Patterns and Conventions Found

**Current model (what exists today):**

The two-directory model is named and described in multiple places, but the *behavior* is mostly already correct. `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` is only written to by `finish-archive-artifacts`. No pipeline skill writes documents to plans/ during active work. The archive step runs inside the worktree before the close step.

**What the design brief is actually asking to fix** is primarily a documentation and framing problem, with one behavioral gap:

1. `chester-config-read.sh:8` — comment says "Two-directory model" and explains both dirs
2. `setup-start/SKILL.md:49-56` — explains both directories as peer concepts to new users, asks them to configure plans/ path during first-run
3. `setup-start/SKILL.md:80-146` — Check 3 verifies plans/ is NOT gitignored (which is correct operationally, but the framing makes plans/ feel like an active workspace to configure)
4. `util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md:29-39` — the config table presents both vars as equally "active" with no emphasis that plans/ is only touched at archive time
5. `execute-verify-complete/SKILL.md:68` — checkpoint commit (`checkpoint: execution complete`) is a code-only marker, not a document commit — this is fine as-is
6. `finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md` — behavior is correct, but it runs inside the worktree (correct) and commits documents there (correct — those commits become permanent record at merge)

**The one structural ambiguity:** `finish-archive-artifacts` is called by `execute-write` before `finish-close-worktree`. The design brief says "archive at merge only." Currently archive happens in the worktree before the merge/PR decision. This means the document commit exists on the feature branch and gets merged in. That IS "at merge time" from the perspective of main — the documents are never on main until the branch is merged. The current behavior satisfies the design intent.

**True gaps requiring changes:**

- The language describing the directory model is inconsistent and misleading
- `setup-start` treats plans/ as something the user actively configures, creating confusion about its purpose
- `chester-config-read.sh` comment describes a "two-directory model" which signals symmetry when the relationship is asymmetric (active vs. archive)
- `util-artifact-schema` presents both variables symmetrically without explaining that plans/ is write-once at archive time

### Architecture Decision

**Chosen approach: Documentation-first simplification with single behavioral change**

The behavior is already 90% correct. The fix is to reframe the mental model in every place it is described, and make the config/setup experience reflect that working/ is the active workspace and plans/ is an output target that only `finish-archive-artifacts` ever touches.

One behavioral change: `setup-start` should stop asking users to configure `plans_dir` as an active path they need to think about. It should present it as "where finished artifacts are committed at merge time" — a consequence of the workflow, not a workspace to configure.

The config script still needs to export `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` (it's a valid path), but the framing in every description changes from "two directories you manage" to "one scratch space you work in, one archive target that fills itself."

**Trade-offs accepted:** This approach avoids changing `chester-config-read.sh` output structure (all skills calling `eval "$(chester-config-read)"` continue to work), avoids any behavior change in archive/close/write-records, and keeps the variable names stable. The only cost is that the descriptions in several files need careful rewriting.

### Component Design

**Files to modify (7 total, no new files needed):**

---

**File 1: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh`**

Responsibility: Single source of truth for path resolution. Currently misnamed conceptually.

Changes:
- Lines 4-8: Replace the comment block. Remove "Two-directory model" framing. Replace with: "working/ — gitignored scratch space for active sprint work. plans/ — archive target populated only by finish-archive-artifacts at merge time."
- The exported variable names stay identical (`CHESTER_WORKING_DIR`, `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR`). No callers change.

---

**File 2: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md`**

Responsibility: Single source of truth for artifact naming, paths, and directory semantics.

Changes:
- Lines 26-39: Rewrite the config resolution section. The two-variable table stays, but the description of `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` changes from "Relative path to tracked plans directory" to "Archive target — populated only by `finish-archive-artifacts` at sprint close". Add a clear note: "Skills write exclusively to `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR` during active work. `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` is never written to directly by pipeline skills."
- Lines 33-39: Rewrite the two-bullet explanation. "Working directory" bullet stays. "Plans directory" bullet changes from describing it as where "finished artifacts are committed alongside code" to making explicit that it receives a single bulk copy from `finish-archive-artifacts` and is never written to incrementally.

---

**File 3: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/setup-start/SKILL.md`**

Responsibility: Session start, first-run configuration, skill registry.

This is the largest change because it is the user's first encounter with the model.

Changes:
- Lines 49-56 (first-run explanation): Rewrite the two-directory explanation. Instead of presenting both directories as peer config choices, frame it as: "Chester uses one scratch space (gitignored) where all active work lives. At the end of a sprint, `finish-archive-artifacts` automatically copies everything into a tracked `plans/` directory alongside your code — you don't write there directly."
- Lines 55, 97 (first-run setup): The first-run flow currently asks the user to configure both paths. Change to: only ask for `working_dir` (the one they might want to customize). `plans_dir` defaults silently and is shown at the end but not prompted for, since it's a consequence rather than an active workspace.
- Lines 80-87, 143-150 (Check 3): Keep Check 3 (plans/ not gitignored) as a silent verification — it's still a valid invariant. But remove any language that makes it feel like the user configured plans/ as a workspace.
- Lines 107-111, 151-156 (config echo): Keep echoing both paths at the end — this is good hygiene — but add a parenthetical to plans/ that makes its role clear: `Plans directory: {CHESTER_PLANS_DIR} (archive — populated at sprint close)`.

The first-run config write (lines ~93-98) still writes both keys to JSON. `plans_dir` goes in with the default value, silently. The user is not asked.

---

**File 4: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/start-bootstrap/SKILL.md`**

Responsibility: Mechanical session setup. Returns sprint context to calling skills.

Changes:
- Lines 81-82 (What It Returns): Change `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` description from "relative path to tracked plans directory" to "archive target path (used only by finish-archive-artifacts)". This prevents any skill that reads bootstrap docs from treating plans/ as a place it might write.

---

**File 5: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md`**

Responsibility: The single writer of plans/. Currently correct behavior, needs description update.

Changes:
- Description (frontmatter): The current description already says "Copy all sprint artifacts from the gitignored working directory into the worktree's tracked plans directory." This is correct. Add to the opening body: "This is the **only** Chester skill that writes to `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR`. All pipeline skills write exclusively to the working directory during active work."
- This explicit "only" statement creates a clear contract visible to anyone reading this skill.

---

**File 6: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md`**

Responsibility: Merge/PR/discard decision and worktree cleanup.

Currently this skill says nothing about when documents enter the git record. The design brief says documents reach git "only when closing the worktree." The current flow is: archive-artifacts (commits docs to feature branch) → close-worktree (merges feature branch). Documents reach main at merge. This is correct.

Changes:
- Add a brief note at the top of the skill (under the first `##` heading, before Step 1): "At this point, `finish-archive-artifacts` has already committed sprint documents to this branch. When you merge (Option 1) or push for a PR (Option 2), those document commits become part of the permanent record. No additional document handling is needed."

This makes the document lifecycle visible at the point where a developer might wonder "did the docs get committed?"

---

**File 7: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/execute-verify-complete/SKILL.md`**

The checkpoint commit (`checkpoint: execution complete`) is a code tree marker, not a document commit. The design brief says "no checkpoint commits of documents." This skill is already correct — it commits an empty marker against code, not documents. No documents exist in the worktree at this point (they're in working/).

Changes: None required. The skill is correct. The confusion would only arise if someone read "checkpoint" and assumed documents were being committed. The skill text is clear ("uses `--allow-empty` because all implementation work is already committed").

No change needed.

---

### Data Flow

**During active sprint work (unchanged):**
```
User request
  → design-figure-out → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/design/
  → design-specify    → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/spec/
  → plan-build        → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/plan/
  → execute-write     → writes code to worktree, writes deferred items to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/plan/
  → execute-verify-complete → empty checkpoint commit (code boundary marker only)
  → finish-write-records    → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/summary/
```

**At sprint close (documents enter git exactly once):**
```
finish-archive-artifacts
  → cp -r CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/ → WORKTREE_ROOT/CHESTER_PLANS_DIR/{sprint}/
  → git add CHESTER_PLANS_DIR/{sprint}/
  → git commit -m "docs: archive sprint artifacts for {sprint-name}"

finish-close-worktree
  → Option 1: git merge --no-ff {feature-branch}  [document commit reaches main]
  → Option 2: git push + gh pr create              [document commit reaches main via PR]
  → Option 3/4: keep/discard                       [document commit stays on branch only]
```

`CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` receives documents exactly once: the `cp -r` in `finish-archive-artifacts`. No skill before that point touches plans/. No skill after that point writes to it.

### Implementation Map

**Phase 1: Config and schema (zero risk, no behavioral change)**

- [ ] Edit `chester-config-read.sh` lines 4-8: replace "Two-directory model" comment block with asymmetric framing (active scratch / archive target)
- [ ] Edit `util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md` lines 29-39: rewrite the config resolution section to name `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` as an archive target and add the explicit "skills write exclusively to working dir" statement
- [ ] Edit `start-bootstrap/SKILL.md` lines 81-82: update `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` description

**Phase 2: Setup experience (moderate change, affects new-project onboarding)**

- [ ] Edit `setup-start/SKILL.md` lines 49-56: rewrite first-run explanation to present single-scratch-space model
- [ ] Edit `setup-start/SKILL.md` lines 55 and 93-98: remove plans_dir prompt from first-run interactive flow; write default silently; show at end with "(archive — populated at sprint close)" label
- [ ] Edit `setup-start/SKILL.md` lines 107-111 and 151-156: update the config echo format to use the new label for plans/ in both first-run and returning-session paths
- [ ] Edit `setup-start/SKILL.md` lines 80-87 and 143-150: keep Check 3 behavior but soften language — it is a silent invariant check, not a user-facing configuration prompt

**Phase 3: Finish sequence documentation**

- [ ] Edit `finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md`: add "only skill that writes to CHESTER_PLANS_DIR" statement to opening body
- [ ] Edit `finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md`: add document lifecycle note before Step 1

**Phase 4: Sync setup-start skill registry**

- [ ] Verify `setup-start/SKILL.md` Available Skills list entries for `finish-archive-artifacts` and `finish-close-worktree` match their updated descriptions — no change needed there since descriptions didn't change
- [ ] CLAUDE.md note: The CLAUDE.md currently says `docs/chester/working/` and `docs/chester/plans/` are both real directories. The `docs/chester/plans/` entry is correct — it's tracked. No CLAUDE.md change needed.

### Critical Details

**What must NOT change:**

- `chester-config-read.sh` output format: `export CHESTER_WORKING_DIR=...`, `export CHESTER_PLANS_DIR=...`, `export CHESTER_MAIN_ROOT=...`. Every skill using `eval "$(chester-config-read)"` depends on this.
- The `settings.chester.local.json` schema: both `working_dir` and `plans_dir` keys stay. Removing `plans_dir` from the JSON would require changing config resolution logic and every skill that reads it.
- `finish-archive-artifacts` behavior: the cp-r + git commit flow is exactly correct and should not be touched.
- The `.gitignore` — `docs/chester/working/` is already listed and correct.

**The setup-start first-run rewrite needs care:**

The current first-run flow asks for both paths interactively. After this change, it asks for only `working_dir`. But it must still write `plans_dir` to the JSON (with the default) so that the `CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH` is not `none` on subsequent sessions, and so Check 0 ("config has both keys") in the returning-session path passes without needing to patch the file. Write the default value silently during first-run config creation.

**The "no checkpoint commits of documents" requirement:**

Already satisfied. `execute-verify-complete` makes a code-only empty commit. Documents live in `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR` (gitignored) until `finish-archive-artifacts`. No skill commits documents mid-pipeline. This is already true; only the framing in `setup-start` and `util-artifact-schema` needs to make it explicit.

**Testing the changes:**

The existing test suite in `tests/` covers config resolution. After making changes:
1. Run `bash tests/test-config-read.sh` (if it exists) to verify the script still exports correct variables
2. Verify `setup-start` first-run flow manually by checking that the JSON written during first-run contains both keys with correct defaults
3. Verify returning-session Check 0 still passes (both keys present in JSON)

**Skill description frontmatter (`description:` fields):**

Only `finish-archive-artifacts` description needs a minor update if you want it to call out its exclusive role. The current description is already accurate. Add "the only skill that writes to plans/" optionally — but the frontmatter description is used by the Skill tool registry for invocation routing, not for contract enforcement, so this is cosmetic.

### Build Sequence Summary

The changes are purely textual — no new files, no new variables, no behavioral changes. The sequence is:

1. `chester-config-read.sh` — comment only, touch first to establish authoritative language
2. `util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md` — rewrite config section, this becomes the canonical description that all other skills reference
3. `start-bootstrap/SKILL.md` — one-line description update, follows from schema
4. `setup-start/SKILL.md` — largest rewrite, the first-run UX and returning-session echo format
5. `finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md` — add "only writer" statement
6. `finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md` — add document lifecycle note

The order matters for consistency: establish the canonical language in the config script and schema first, then propagate it outward to user-facing skills.

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh`

Design an architecture for the following change that optimizes for maintainability and clarity. Use clean abstractions, clear boundaries, and future-proof the design — even if it means more upfront work.

## Context

This is the Chester project at /home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester — a skill-based development workflow framework for Claude Code. Skills are Markdown files in skills/ that define workflows. A config script (chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh) exports directory paths. Skills write design documents, specs, plans, and summaries to directories on disk.

## Design Brief Summary

Chester's artifact directory model needs to be simplified:

1. **Single write target**: All skills write exclusively to `docs/chester/working/{sprint}/` (gitignored). No skill writes to `plans/` during active work.
2. **Worktree is for code only**: No documents committed to worktree until merge time.
3. **Archive at merge only**: Documents copied from `working/{sprint}/` to worktree's `plans/{sprint}/` only when closing the worktree.
4. **No checkpoint commits of documents**: Eliminate mid-pipeline checkpoint commits in worktree.
5. **Working/ is user-managed scratch**: No automated cleanup.
6. **Plans/ is the permanent git record**: Created only at merge time.

## What to analyze

Read these files to understand the current state:
- chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh (config system)
- skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md (current artifact schema)
- skills/design-figure-out/SKILL.md (writes design artifacts, creates worktree)
- skills/design-specify/SKILL.md (writes specs)
- skills/plan-build/SKILL.md (writes plans)
- skills/execute-write/SKILL.md (executes plans)
- skills/finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md (archive step)
- skills/finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md (merge step)
- skills/finish-write-records/SKILL.md (writes summaries)
- .gitignore

Provide a complete blueprint: which files need to change, what changes in each, patterns to follow, and a build sequence. Optimize for maintainability — clean abstractions, single source of truth for path logic, clear separation of concerns between skills.

- Lines 107-111, 151-156 (config echo): Keep echoing both paths at the end — this is good hygiene — but add a parenthetical to plans/ that makes its role clear: `Plans directory: {CHESTER_PLANS_DIR} (archive — populated at sprint close)`.

The first-run config write (lines ~93-98) still writes both keys to JSON. `plans_dir` goes in with the default value, silently. The user is not asked.

---

**File 4: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/start-bootstrap/SKILL.md`**

Responsibility: Mechanical session setup. Returns sprint context to calling skills.

Changes:
- Lines 81-82 (What It Returns): Change `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` description from "relative path to tracked plans directory" to "archive target path (used only by finish-archive-artifacts)". This prevents any skill that reads bootstrap docs from treating plans/ as a place it might write.

---

**File 5: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md`**

Responsibility: The single writer of plans/. Currently correct behavior, needs description update.

Changes:
- Description (frontmatter): The current description already says "Copy all sprint artifacts from the gitignored working directory into the worktree's tracked plans directory." This is correct. Add to the opening body: "This is the **only** Chester skill that writes to `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR`. All pipeline skills write exclusively to the working directory during active work."
- This explicit "only" statement creates a clear contract visible to anyone reading this skill.

---

**File 6: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md`**

Responsibility: Merge/PR/discard decision and worktree cleanup.

Currently this skill says nothing about when documents enter the git record. The design brief says documents reach git "only when closing the worktree." The current flow is: archive-artifacts (commits docs to feature branch) → close-worktree (merges feature branch). Documents reach main at merge. This is correct.

Changes:
- Add a brief note at the top of the skill (under the first `##` heading, before Step 1): "At this point, `finish-archive-artifacts` has already committed sprint documents to this branch. When you merge (Option 1) or push for a PR (Option 2), those document commits become part of the permanent record. No additional document handling is needed."

This makes the document lifecycle visible at the point where a developer might wonder "did the docs get committed?"

---

**File 7: `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/execute-verify-complete/SKILL.md`**

The checkpoint commit (`checkpoint: execution complete`) is a code tree marker, not a document commit. The design brief says "no checkpoint commits of documents." This skill is already correct — it commits an empty marker against code, not documents. No documents exist in the worktree at this point (they're in working/).

Changes: None required. The skill is correct. The confusion would only arise if someone read "checkpoint" and assumed documents were being committed. The skill text is clear ("uses `--allow-empty` because all implementation work is already committed").

No change needed.

---

### Data Flow

**During active sprint work (unchanged):**
```
User request
  → design-figure-out → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/design/
  → design-specify    → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/spec/
  → plan-build        → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/plan/
  → execute-write     → writes code to worktree, writes deferred items to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/plan/
  → execute-verify-complete → empty checkpoint commit (code boundary marker only)
  → finish-write-records    → writes to CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/summary/
```

**At sprint close (documents enter git exactly once):**
```
finish-archive-artifacts
  → cp -r CHESTER_WORKING_DIR/{sprint}/ → WORKTREE_ROOT/CHESTER_PLANS_DIR/{sprint}/
  → git add CHESTER_PLANS_DIR/{sprint}/
  → git commit -m "docs: archive sprint artifacts for {sprint-name}"

finish-close-worktree
  → Option 1: git merge --no-ff {feature-branch}  [document commit reaches main]
  → Option 2: git push + gh pr create              [document commit reaches main via PR]
  → Option 3/4: keep/discard                       [document commit stays on branch only]
```

`CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` receives documents exactly once: the `cp -r` in `finish-archive-artifacts`. No skill before that point touches plans/. No skill after that point writes to it.

### Implementation Map

**Phase 1: Config and schema (zero risk, no behavioral change)**

- [ ] Edit `chester-config-read.sh` lines 4-8: replace "Two-directory model" comment block with asymmetric framing (active scratch / archive target)
- [ ] Edit `util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md` lines 29-39: rewrite the config resolution section to name `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` as an archive target and add the explicit "skills write exclusively to working dir" statement
- [ ] Edit `start-bootstrap/SKILL.md` lines 81-82: update `CHESTER_PLANS_DIR` description

**Phase 2: Setup experience (moderate change, affects new-project onboarding)**

- [ ] Edit `setup-start/SKILL.md` lines 49-56: rewrite first-run explanation to present single-scratch-space model
- [ ] Edit `setup-start/SKILL.md` lines 55 and 93-98: remove plans_dir prompt from first-run interactive flow; write default silently; show at end with "(archive — populated at sprint close)" label
- [ ] Edit `setup-start/SKILL.md` lines 107-111 and 151-156: update the config echo format to use the new label for plans/ in both first-run and returning-session paths
- [ ] Edit `setup-start/SKILL.md` lines 80-87 and 143-150: keep Check 3 behavior but soften language — it is a silent invariant check, not a user-facing configuration prompt

**Phase 3: Finish sequence documentation**

- [ ] Edit `finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md`: add "only skill that writes to CHESTER_PLANS_DIR" statement to opening body
- [ ] Edit `finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md`: add document lifecycle note before Step 1

**Phase 4: Sync setup-start skill registry**

- [ ] Verify `setup-start/SKILL.md` Available Skills list entries for `finish-archive-artifacts` and `finish-close-worktree` match their updated descriptions — no change needed there since descriptions didn't change
- [ ] CLAUDE.md note: The CLAUDE.md currently says `docs/chester/working/` and `docs/chester/plans/` are both real directories. The `docs/chester/plans/` entry is correct — it's tracked. No CLAUDE.md change needed.

### Critical Details

**What must NOT change:**

- `chester-config-read.sh` output format: `export CHESTER_WORKING_DIR=...`, `export CHESTER_PLANS_DIR=...`, `export CHESTER_MAIN_ROOT=...`. Every skill using `eval "$(chester-config-read)"` depends on this.
- The `settings.chester.local.json` schema: both `working_dir` and `plans_dir` keys stay. Removing `plans_dir` from the JSON would require changing config resolution logic and every skill that reads it.
- `finish-archive-artifacts` behavior: the cp-r + git commit flow is exactly correct and should not be touched.
- The `.gitignore` — `docs/chester/working/` is already listed and correct.

**The setup-start first-run rewrite needs care:**

The current first-run flow asks for both paths interactively. After this change, it asks for only `working_dir`. But it must still write `plans_dir` to the JSON (with the default) so that the `CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH` is not `none` on subsequent sessions, and so Check 0 ("config has both keys") in the returning-session path passes without needing to patch the file. Write the default value silently during first-run config creation.

**The "no checkpoint commits of documents" requirement:**

Already satisfied. `execute-verify-complete` makes a code-only empty commit. Documents live in `CHESTER_WORKING_DIR` (gitignored) until `finish-archive-artifacts`. No skill commits documents mid-pipeline. This is already true; only the framing in `setup-start` and `util-artifact-schema` needs to make it explicit.

**Testing the changes:**

The existing test suite in `tests/` covers config resolution. After making changes:
1. Run `bash tests/test-config-read.sh` (if it exists) to verify the script still exports correct variables
2. Verify `setup-start` first-run flow manually by checking that the JSON written during first-run contains both keys with correct defaults
3. Verify returning-session Check 0 still passes (both keys present in JSON)

**Skill description frontmatter (`description:` fields):**

Only `finish-archive-artifacts` description needs a minor update if you want it to call out its exclusive role. The current description is already accurate. Add "the only skill that writes to plans/" optionally — but the frontmatter description is used by the Skill tool registry for invocation routing, not for contract enforcement, so this is cosmetic.

### Build Sequence Summary

The changes are purely textual — no new files, no new variables, no behavioral changes. The sequence is:

1. `chester-config-read.sh` — comment only, touch first to establish authoritative language
2. `util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md` — rewrite config section, this becomes the canonical description that all other skills reference
3. `start-bootstrap/SKILL.md` — one-line description update, follows from schema
4. `setup-start/SKILL.md` — largest rewrite, the first-run UX and returning-session echo format
5. `finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md` — add "only writer" statement
6. `finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md` — add document lifecycle note

The order matters for consistency: establish the canonical language in the config script and schema first, then propagate it outward to user-facing skills.

**Relevant files for implementation:**

- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh`
- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md`
- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/start-bootstrap/SKILL.md`
- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/setup-start/SKILL.md`
- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/finish-archive-artifacts/SKILL.md`
- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/finish-close-worktree/SKILL.md`
- `/home/mike/Documents/CodeProjects/Chester/skills/execute-verify-complete/SKILL.md` (no change needed, confirmed correct)

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
All three architects independently concluded the same thing: the mechanical behavior is already correct. Every pipeline skill already writes to working/ and says "finish-archive-artifacts handles archival." The problem is purely in how the model is *described* — the framing in setup-start, util-artifact-schema, and the config script teaches a symmetric two-directory model when the reality is asymmetric (active scratch + merge-time archive).
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

Here's the comparison:

## Three Approaches

### Approach 1: Minimal (2 files)
Only touches `setup-start/SKILL.md` and `util-artifact-schema/SKILL.md`. Adds one sentence to each — "Documents are copied here only at merge time" and "No skill writes to this directory during active sprint work." Verifies all other files are already correct.

### Approach 2: Clean (6-7 files)
Updates every file that describes the directory model: `chester-config-read.sh` comment, `util-artifact-schema`, `setup-start`, `start-bootstrap`, `finish-archive-artifacts` (adds "only writer" contract statement), `finish-close-worktree` (adds document lifecycle note). Also changes setup-start behavior: stops prompting for `plans_dir` during first-run, writes the default silently.

### Approach 3: Pragmatic (3 files)
Hits the three authority files: `chester-config-read.sh` (comment), `util-artifact-schema` (schema rewrite), `setup-start` (first-run explanation). Establishes canonical language at the source of truth and lets existing correct language in downstream skills stand.

| | Minimal | Clean | Pragmatic |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Files changed** | 2 | 6-7 | 3 |
| **Change surface** | ~4 lines | ~60 lines | ~20 lines |
| **Maintainability** | Leaves fragmented descriptions | Comprehensive, consistent everywhere | Consistent at authority level |
| **Risk** | Low | Moderate (behavioral change to setup) | Low |
| **Future-proofing** | Weak — new skill author could still misread the model | Strong — contract statements everywhere | Good — schema is clear, downstream skills already correct |

**My recommendation:** Approach 3 (Pragmatic). The schema and config script are where skills and LLMs go to learn the model. Getting those right propagates the correct understanding without touching files that already say the right thing. Approach 1 is too thin — it doesn't fix the config script comment that says "Two-directory model." Approach 2 adds contract statements to finish-archive-artifacts and finish-close-worktree that are nice but not load-bearing since those skills already behave correctly.

Which approach, or a hybrid?
Now let me read the three files that need to change and write the spec.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# chester-config-read.sh — Resolve layered Chester config
# Usage: eval "$($CHESTER_ROOT/chester-util-config/chester-config-read.sh)"
# Exports: CHESTER_WORKING_DIR, CHESTER_PLANS_DIR, CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH
#
# Two-directory model:
#   CHESTER_WORKING_DIR — absolute path, gitignored, for active design/spec/plan work
#   CHESTER_PLANS_DIR   — relative to repo root, tracked in git, for final artifacts merged with code

set -euo pipefail

# Anchor to the MAIN worktree root (not a child worktree)
MAIN_ROOT="$(git worktree list --porcelain 2>/dev/null | head -1 | sed 's/^worktree //')"
if [ -z "$MAIN_ROOT" ]; then
  MAIN_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)"
fi

# Defaults (single source of truth)
DEFAULT_WORKING_DIR="docs/chester/working"
DEFAULT_PLANS_DIR="docs/chester/plans"

# Config locations (Claude Code convention)
PROJECT_CONFIG="$MAIN_ROOT/.claude/settings.chester.local.json"
USER_CONFIG="$HOME/.claude/settings.chester.json"

# --- Config resolution ---
if command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
  if [ -f "$PROJECT_CONFIG" ]; then
    # Directory paths come ONLY from project config (never user config)
    CHESTER_WORKING_DIR=$(jq -r '.working_dir // "'"$DEFAULT_WORKING_DIR"'"' "$PROJECT_CONFIG")
    CHESTER_PLANS_DIR=$(jq -r '.plans_dir // "'"$DEFAULT_PLANS_DIR"'"' "$PROJECT_CONFIG")
    CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH="$PROJECT_CONFIG"
  elif [ -f "$USER_CONFIG" ]; then
    # User config exists but has no directory authority — use defaults
    CHESTER_WORKING_DIR="$DEFAULT_WORKING_DIR"
    CHESTER_PLANS_DIR="$DEFAULT_PLANS_DIR"
    CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH="$USER_CONFIG"
  else
    CHESTER_WORKING_DIR="$DEFAULT_WORKING_DIR"
    CHESTER_PLANS_DIR="$DEFAULT_PLANS_DIR"
    CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH="none"
  fi
else
  CHESTER_WORKING_DIR="$DEFAULT_WORKING_DIR"
  CHESTER_PLANS_DIR="$DEFAULT_PLANS_DIR"
  CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH="none"
  echo "# Chester: jq not available, using defaults" >&2
fi

# Resolve working_dir to absolute path (anchored to main worktree root)
case "$CHESTER_WORKING_DIR" in
  /*) ;; # already absolute
  *)  CHESTER_WORKING_DIR="$MAIN_ROOT/$CHESTER_WORKING_DIR" ;;
esac

echo "export CHESTER_WORKING_DIR='$CHESTER_WORKING_DIR'"
echo "export CHESTER_PLANS_DIR='$CHESTER_PLANS_DIR'"
echo "export CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH='$CHESTER_CONFIG_PATH'"
echo "export CHESTER_MAIN_ROOT='$MAIN_ROOT'"
